CONTENT.Club career: Kamara is a youth exponent of Châteauroux. He made his Ligue 2 debut for the club on 4 April 2014 in a match against Clermont. On 2 May 2014, he scored his first league goal in a match against Laval.In August 2015, Kamara signed with Reims before joining Paris FC on loan. He helped Reims win the 2017–18 Ligue 2, achieving promotion to Ligue 1 for the 2018–19 season.On 26 June 2020, Kamara signed with Nice for an undisclosed fee reported to be around €4 million euros.On 4 January 2022, Kamara signed for English club Watford on a contract until June 2025. He scored his first Watford goal in the 5-1 defeat to the league leaders Manchester City. On 7 May 2022, in the game against their manager Roy Hodgson's return to Selhurst Park, Kamara was sent off by two yellow cards after in the first half he gave away a penalty due to a handball. His team would go on to lose 1-0 against Crystal Palace and be relegated from the Premier League for the fourth time.On 23 August 2022, Kamara signed for Serie A club Udinese for an undisclosed fee, returning on loan to Watford for the 2022–23 season.
CONTENT.External links: Hassane Kamara at SoccerwayHassane Kamara at National-Football-Teams.com
CONTENT.International career: Kamara holds French, Malian, Gambian and Ivorian nationalities. He rejected a callup to the Gambia national team in March 2017. He debuted for the Ivory Coast national team in a friendly 2–1 win over Burkina Faso on 5 June 2021.

SUMMARY: Hassane Kamara (born 5 March 1994) is a professional footballer who plays as a left back or left wing-back for Serie A club Udinese. Born in France, he plays for the Ivory Coast national team.
